title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot follows Jeronimo to Mrs. Habert's room, where she provides him with a written timeline of recent events. Mrs. Habert explains that Mrs. Nicolaitis was recently distressed by a police search that resulted in the discovery of numerous empty brandy bottles after an officer forced open her cabinet.

While reviewing the timeline, Poirot notes that a canvas bag was listed as the first incident, occurring before the jewelry thefts began. Mrs. Habert recalls that the bag belonged to Lennon Bates and was eventually found cut into pieces behind the boiler. This incident coincided with the police questioning an African student regarding "immoral income," an event that occurred on the same day a lightbulb went missing from the public lounge. Jeronimo had previously reported that all spare bulbs were missing, forcing the residents to use candles.

Poirot investigates the nature of the damaged property and views a backpack in Colin MacNab's room that is nearly identical to Lennon's destroyed bag. He observes that the bag would have required significant strength and malice to cut, rather than simple scissors. Mrs. Habert also mentions that Valerie's scarf was found cut into pieces.

After discussing the timeline of thefts, which began around late February, Poirot examines the view from the balcony. He identifies the location of the boiler room near the coal shed and observes the layout of the house, noting that the rooms of Colin MacNab, Nigel Chapman, and Lennon Bates face the back garden, situated near the girls' rooms.
